title: Paathu Paathu Lyrics Manja Pai Song Lyrics
stanzas:
Song: Paathu Paathu
Singer: Hariharasudhan , Vandana Srinivasan
Movie: Manja Pai
Music: N.R. Raghunanthan
Lyricist: Yugabharathi

Paathu Paathu unna paathu
vaanam kuttaiyaachu.
Poothu poothu kannum poothu
boomi thattayaachu.

Unna paathu thaaney
nizhalum vellai aachu.
Aasa koodi poga
anilum singam aachu.

Thaali seyya sollu neeyum
thevayilla vetti pechu .

Paathu Paathu unna paathu
vaanam kuttaiyaachu.
Poothu poothu kannum poothu
boomi thattayaachu.

Kozhi rekka unna paarthu
vaanavillu aanen.
Konja neram unna pesi
kannadhaasan aanen.

Kooda pandhu neeyum theenda
boomi pandhu aanen.
Kooru kaththi kangalaala
oodhupathi aanen.

Noolu gandu unnaal, koli gundu aanen.
Paadha mannu yeno, poosum manjal aanen.
Jodiyaaga neeyum sera
pattu poochi pattam aanen.

Paathu Paathu unna paathu
vaanam kuttaiyaachu.
Poothu poothu kannum poothu
boomi thattayaachu.

Nethu unna paartha pinbu
thoogalaanu ponen.
Kanavil neeyum thurathi adikka
thorthuthaana ponen.

Thothu neeyum podurenu
kobamaagi ponen.
konjidaama poniyenu
saapudaama ponen.

Unna ennai naaney, olliyaagi ponen.
pulli neeyum vaikka kolamaagi ponen.
Koodu vittu koodu paaya
khokku maakku aagi ponen.

Paathu Paathu unna paathu
vaanam kuttaiyaachu.
Poothu poothu kannum poothu
boomi thattayaachu.

Unna paathu thaaney
nizhalum vellai aachu.
Aasa koodi poga
anilum singam aachu.

Thaali seyya sollu neeyum
thevayilla vetti pechu .

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song 'Paathu Paathu'?

The song 'Paathu Paathu' celebrates joy and the beauty of life, highlighting moments of happiness and togetherness.

What is the plot context of the movie 'Manjapai'?

The movie 'Manjapai' revolves around the themes of tradition and modernity, showcasing the bond between a grandfather and his grandson.

Who are the singers of the song?

The song features the talented voices of Hariharasudhan and Vandhana Srinivasan.

Who composed the music for 'Paathu Paathu'?

The music for 'Paathu Paathu' was composed by N. R. Raghunanthan.

When was 'Paathu Paathu' released?

'Paathu Paathu' was released on April 8, 2014.
enrichedMetadata.summary: In the movie Manjapai, the song Paathu Paathu serves as a joyful anthem that encapsulates the essence of celebration and togetherness. The lively lyrics and upbeat melody evoke feelings of happiness, making it a perfect fit for festive occasions. As the characters revel in life's simple pleasures, the song beautifully conveys the message of cherishing moments with loved ones. Its infectious energy and vibrant composition leave listeners feeling uplifted and inspired.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Paathu Paathu from Manjapai is a vibrant celebration of life and joy, brought to life by the enchanting voices of Hariharasudhan and Vandhana Srinivasan. With N. R. Raghunanthan's lively composition, this song perfectly captures the essence of happiness and togetherness. Dive into this delightful melody that promises to uplift your spirits and make every moment special!
stanzaMeanings: Look, look, the sky has changed. Flowers have bloomed, and the earth has been touched.
Looking at you, even the shadow has turned white. With hopes rising, the wind has become a lion.
You don't need to say anything to tie the knot; there's no need for unnecessary talk.
Seeing you, I became a star in the sky. After a while, I became a friend who talks to you.
Together, we became a part of the earth. With a sharp knife, I became a guide with my eyes.
With your thread, I became a chicken. With the soil of the path, I became a yellow flower. Together, you and I became a beautiful butterfly.
After seeing you yesterday, I couldn't sleep. In my dreams, you were there, making me feel alive.
I got angry thinking you would leave me. I wondered if I could eat without you.
I became you, and I became a part of you. I became a thorn, and I became a flower. Leaving the nest, I became a bird flying high.
Looking at you, even the shadow has turned white. With hopes rising, the wind has become a lion.
You don't need to say anything to tie the knot; there's no need for unnecessary talk.
